km.Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England: ប៊ីuដិសេរត, វ៉ារវីកស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre於大約1100年出生在英國瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特。她於大約1130年與John de la Warre在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England: 英国瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre于大约1100年出生在英国瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特。她于大约1130年与John de la Warre在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾波奧德瑟特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.

variants.name: Ware
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre was born about 1100, in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England. She married John de la Warre about 1130, in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre est née environ 1100, à Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John de la Warre environ 1130, à Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre wurde um 1100 in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1130 in Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom John de la Warre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre è nata il circa 1100 nel Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England. Lei sposato John de la Warre circa 1130, nel Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Beaudesert, Warwickshire, England: イギリスワーウィクシュアイアビーウデセルト
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warreは約1100年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアビーウデセルトで生まれた。約1130年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアビーウデセルトでJohn de la Warreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre(은)는 대략 1100년 영국 워윅실르 보데세르트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1130년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 보데세르트에서 John de la Warre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre nasceu aproximadamente 1100, em Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John de la Warre aproximadamente 1130, em Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre родилась около 1100 г. в местности бодесирт, варвиккшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de la Warre около 1130 г. в местности бодесирт, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. John de la Warre nació aproximadamente 1100, en Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con John de la Warre aproximadamente 1130, en Beaudesert, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
